discovery.text: Verified for Gran Vía: Círculo de Bellas Artes sits just off the avenue at Alcalá and gives the street one of its strongest cultural spillovers — rooftop, cinema, exhibitions, and late hours that keep the boulevard legible as public life rather than pure traffic or retail frontage.

narrative.summary: A major civic-cultural edge one block off Gran Vía, with current exhibition and cinema programming that extends the street's evening life.

blocks.title: Why it teaches the street
blocks.body: Gran Vía works partly because its monumental frontage is backed by real destinations. Círculo de Bellas Artes shows that depth: an institution with galleries, screening rooms, rooftop views, and long operating hours feeding pedestrians back onto the avenue.

blocks.title: Current signal
blocks.body: The official programme currently lists La lechuza de Minerva and Eclosión exhibitions through 10 May 2026, plus ongoing cinema screenings — a strong sign this edge remains actively programmed now, not just architecturally important.
